problem_summary: Arnos Bistrot has built an exceptional online reputation with a 4.9-star rating across 293 Google reviews, yet this credibility is not currently being surfaced dynamically on the website itself. The mobile PageSpeed score of 65 creates friction at the moment tourist and local visitors are most likely to be searching on their phones, and the absence of any fresh content limits the site's ability to appear for a broader range of local and seasonal dining queries. Targeted local SEO improvements would help convert existing organic visibility into more direct bookings.
recommended_solution: A focused Local SEO Starter engagement covering schema markup implementation, dynamic Google Reviews embedding, and a structured local content plan. Phase 1 addresses the technical and trust signal foundations, while Phase 2 delivers a practical content framework targeting Westport and Mayo dining queries.
scope_of_work: Add Restaurant and LocalBusiness schema markup to the homepage covering location, opening hours, and review aggregate to improve appearance in local search results for Westport and Mayo dining queries.
Embed a dynamic Google Reviews widget to automatically surface the 4.9-star rating and most recent verified reviews on-site, replacing the current manually maintained testimonials display.

Deliver a lightweight local content plan outlining four to six post topics per quarter targeting relevant local search intent, including recommended page structure and on-page copy guidance to support implementation without requiring significant time investment from the team.
expected_outcomes: Schema markup should improve how the business appears in local search results, making key information such as location, hours, and review aggregate more visible to users searching for Westport dining options.
Dynamically embedded Google Reviews is likely to strengthen on-site trust signals for first-time visitors who are undecided, reducing the gap between discovery and a confirmed reservation.

A structured content plan should support improved crawl frequency and local search relevance over time, helping the site appear for a broader range of seasonal and location-based queries beyond the homepage.
next_steps: Schedule a 20-minute review call to walk through the proposed scope, confirm any unknowns around the current booking system and schema setup, and agree a start date for Phase 1.
risks_if_delayed: Each week without schema markup and dynamic reviews in place is a missed opportunity to convert the strong Google reputation into on-site credibility for undecided visitors. As local search competition in the Westport hospitality market continues to develop, establishing this foundation sooner supports a more durable long-term position.
